mnemonics.title: Ternary Syntax Remember
mnemonics.content: Think of '?' as 'Is it true?' and ':' as 'Otherwise, use this'. Like asking a question and responding based on the answer.

mnemonics.title: Three Parts of Ternary
mnemonics.content: Remember: CONDITION ? IF_TRUE : IF_FALSE - Think of it as 'Question Mark Colon' Q?T:F pattern

acronyms.usage: To remember that it requires three parts: condition, true expression, false expression

acronyms.expansion: Question mark then True, colon then False
acronyms.usage: Remember the syntax order: Question Mark, True value, Colon, False value

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a fork in the road: condition is the signpost, '?' is the decision point, left path is true (T), right path is false (F)
Think of ternary as a 'question that must answer' - it gives back a value, while if-else just 'does' things without returning

analogies.analogy: It's like a vending machine: you insert a condition (coin), press the button (?), and get either drink A or B based on your selection.
Like choosing between two routes: if it's raining (? true), take umbrella route; otherwise (: false), take normal route - all in one decision.
